t_ch: （幽灵小姐，其实我们也可以试试让你进电视……）
t_en: (Miss Ghost, we could also try getting you into the TV...)

t_ch: 电视机…到电视机里去……
t_en: The TV... into the TV...

t_ch: （额，我的意思是，{NICKNAME}一定可以！对吧？）
t_en: (Uhh, I mean, {NICKNAME} can definitely do it! Right?)

t_ch: 喂！不要随便替我答应啊！
t_en: Hey! Don't go making promises on my behalf!

t_ch: 话虽如此，在两条小狗瑟瑟发抖的注视之下，你终究只能再度点了点头。毕竟，责任重大，小狗的溜肩扛不住，还是交给你这个唯一的人类吧！
t_en: Despite it, you find yourself defenseless against the trembling gaze of the two puppies, and can only nod your head in response. After all, it's a heavy responsibility on a puppy's shoulders... better leave it to the only human around!

t_ch: 问题的核心，似乎都在这台电视机上……
t_en: The heart of the problem seems to be this TV...

t_ch: （不愧是{NICKNAME}！）
t_en: (I knew you got this, {NICKNAME}!)

t_ch: （拍一拍电视机顶）
t_en: (Pat the top of the TV.)

t_ch: （如果合成机出了什么故障，姬子好像就是这么修理的…）
t_en: (Himeko did this to the Synthesizer whenever it malfunctioned...)

t_ch: （扭一扭电视机钮）
t_en: (Turn the TV knob.)

t_ch: （都宇宙时代了，这电视上怎么还有实体旋钮？哇哦，这该不会是个微波炉吧？）
t_en: (It's the space age, why does this TV still have physical knobs? Whoa, don't tell me this is actually a microwave?)

t_ch: （等等，{NICKNAME}！！不要把佩佩一条狗留在……）
t_en: ({NICKNAME}, wait! Don't leave Peppy all—...)

t_ch: 佩佩的声音逐渐远去，你甚至来不及反应，就被一阵嘈杂的电流音彻底吞噬「嗞——嗞——」
t_en: Peppy's voice fades away, and before you even know it, a deafening burst of static noise completely engulfs you. "Bzzt— Bzzt—"

t_ch: 欢迎大家收看本期《请变幸福吧》！{RUBY_B#欢愉}幸福{RUBY_E#}的真意，你来定义！有请我们今天的待改造嘉宾——
t_en: Welcome to today's episode of Please Be Happy, where YOU define what {RUBY_B#Elation}HAPPINESS{RUBY_E#} truly means! And now, please welcome our guest awaiting transformation...

t_ch: <size=28>不会笑的珀葵小姐！！</size>
t_en: <size=28>The Unsmiling Miss Bequea!</size>

t_ch: 悲伤的过往淹没她的欢笑，不会笑的珀葵小姐等待着有人能将她重新领回哈哈大笑的欢愉之道。让我们看看，今天又是哪位乐子专家将对悲伤的她发起疗愈挑战呢！
t_en: A sorrowful past has drowned her laughter. Now, the Unsmiling Miss Bequea waits for someone to guide her back onto the path of joy and hearty laughter. Let's see which cheermaker will take up the healing challenge for our sad guest today!

t_ch: 没错就是我！扮演凶手的专业户@独自躺赢！
t_en: That's right! It's me, @Lone_Freerider, the professional culprit-actor extraordinaire!

t_ch: 作为被各大侦探剧组邀约出演，炙手可热的凶手专业户，我将带来我的拿手把戏：一百种被捕时的惊慌搞笑之状！
t_en: As a highly sought-after professional culprit-actor with multiple major detective show appearances, I shall present my specialty: <unbreak>100</unbreak> Ways to Look Hilariously Panicked When Getting Arrested!

t_ch: 哎呀，太可惜了，珀葵小姐并没有哈哈大笑！这谁找来的…有请下一位乐子专家！
t_en: Oh, what a shame. Miss Bequea didn't burst out laughing! Who invited this one... Next cheermaker, please!

t_ch: 我是鸽川小子！
t_en: I'm Dovebrook Kid!

t_ch: 我是海原小子！
t_en: And I'm Seafeld Kid!

t_ch: 今天我们为大家带来二维市八大不可思议之——
t_en: Today, we bring you the Eight Mysteries of Duomension City...

t_ch: 不要和我们对视！
t_en: Don't look at us!

t_ch: 哎？
t_en: Huh?

t_ch: 不要和我们对视，因为我们的笑容，会传染！
t_en: Avoid making eye contact with us, because our smiles are contagious!

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈哈哈哈
t_en: Hahahahaha

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈哈哈哈
t_en: Hahahahaha

t_ch: 哈，哈，哈，这……
t_en: Hah, ha, ha, this...

t_ch: 不好笑吗？
t_en: Is it not funny?

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈哈哈哈，珀葵笑了。
t_en: Hahahahaha, Bequea's smiling.

t_ch: 诶？
t_en: Huh?

t_ch: 哈哈哈！珀葵，笑啊！
t_en: Hahaha! Come on Bequea, smile!

t_ch: 哈哈哈！珀葵，笑啊！
t_en: Hahaha! Come on Bequea, smile!

s_ch: 鸽川小子&海原小子&主持人
t_ch: 珀葵，快笑啊！
t_en: Bequea, smile!

t_ch: 珀葵——笑啊！！！
t_en: Bequea... SMILE!!!

t_ch: <size=36>挑战成功！！！</size>
t_en: <size=36>CHALLENGE SUCCESS!!!</size>

t_ch: 咔！！！到底是谁的主意？找来个演凶手的什么…躺赢专业户？这不专戳人伤疤吗！
t_en: Cut!!! Whose bright idea was this? Getting some... "Lone Freerider" to play the culprit? That's incredibly insensitive!

t_ch: 我安排的，制片人选个专家的权力总有吧！不过你给人嘉宾安个「不会笑的珀葵小姐」人设又是个什么意思？
t_en: I arranged it. I have the right to pick cheermakers as a producer! And you, dubbing our guest the "Unsmiling Miss Bequea." What's that about?

t_ch: 作为导演，我当然是想让她安心在这治疗创伤啊，用个人设，忘掉「那件事」，不就想让她好受点。
t_en: As the director, of course I want her to heal her trauma here comfortably. Having a persona helps her forget about "the incident," and makes things easier on the poor girl.

t_ch: 我不认可啊，战胜痛苦最好的方法就是面对它，人姑娘可比你想的勇敢多了！
t_en: I disagree. The best way to overcome pain is to face it head-on. She is much tougher than you give her credit for!

t_ch: 我跟你这个势利鬼就说不到一块儿去！不拍了！
t_en: There's no reasoning with that thick, snobby skull of yours! That's it, the shoot's over!

t_ch: 能不能冷静下来？谁惯你这臭脾气！
t_en: Would you calm your pants? Everyone's had enough of your tantrums!

t_ch: （如果我没认错脸，这个珀葵应该就是外面那个幽灵生前的样子，那这里……）
t_en: (If I'm not mistaken, this Bequea must be that ghost when she was still alive. So this place is...)

t_ch: 「吧嗒吧嗒——」一阵纷乱急躁的小狗爪击音突然在你耳边响起。
t_en: "Tap, tap-tap—" You suddenly hear the frantic pitter-patter of puppy paws.

t_ch: （{NICKNAME}，还好吗，听得到吗？）
t_en: ({NICKNAME}, are you okay? Can you hear me?)

t_ch: 佩佩？你在哪？
t_en: Peppy? Where are you?

t_ch: 这是怎么回事？
t_en: What's going on?

t_ch: （我现在在电视外面！刚刚，你唰一下就被吞进电视机肚子里去了！）
t_en: (I'm outside the TV! Just now, you got sucked right into it!)

t_ch: （现在情况怎么样？）
t_en: (What's the situation?)

t_ch: 感觉这里是个片场…
t_en: Feels like I'm on a set...

t_ch: 是珀葵曾经拍节目的地方。
t_en: This is where Bequea filmed a show.

t_ch: （弄不清状况的话，要不我们找人聊聊？）
t_en: (Should we talk to someone to figure out what's going on?)

t_ch: 抱歉，我正在准备接下来的拍摄，待会儿再聊吧。
t_en: Sorry, I'm preparing for the next shoot. Let's chat later.

t_ch: 你，对，就你，在那拖拖拉拉消极怠工吗！眼里没活，谁把你招进来的？
t_en: Hey, you! Yeah, you! Dragging your feet and slacking off over there! Can't you see we're all busy? Who hired you anyway?

t_ch: 你左右看了看，周围除了你哪还有第三个人。好吧，看来他指责的对象只能是你了。
t_en: You glance around, and there's not a single soul in sight except yourself. Looks like there's no dodging out of this one.

t_ch: ——{NICKNAME}•偷懒摸鱼正撞见上司发火的倒霉员工。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, an unlucky employee caught slacking off by the fuming boss.

t_ch: 我问你话呢！
t_en: Hey, I'm talking to you!

t_ch: 您不在，我哪有心思干活！
t_en: I can't focus on work when you're not around!

t_ch: 对不起，我这就开始干活！
t_en: Sorry, I'll get back to it right now!

t_ch: （不错嘛{NICKNAME}，很快就抓到角色精髓了！）
t_en: (Not bad, {NICKNAME}. You've already grasped the essence of your character!)

t_ch: …唉，我也不是冲着你发火，只是这几天情绪实在不好，你别放在心上。
t_en: *sigh* Look, I'm not trying to take it out on you. I've just been in a bad mood these past few days. Don't take it personally.

t_ch: 你也看得出来，这节目做得真让人恼火。但没办法，一个导演就是一个节目的灵魂嘛！其实我也不是不乐意拍，但你应该看出来了，我实在是个善人！
t_en: You see how frustrating this show is to make, but what can you do... the director is the soul of the show after all! It's not that I'm unwilling to film it, but as you see, I'm just too much of a nice person!

t_ch: 你说那小姑娘，才多大就眼睁睁瞧着爹妈死在了告死魔手上，她是为了治愈自己，找回真正的幸福才来这个节目的。难道我要听那个制片人的鬼话，在节目里反复戳她的伤疤？
t_en: That poor girl was just a child when she saw her parents die at the hands of the Herald of Death. She came to this show to heal herself and find true happiness again. Am I really supposed to listen to that producer's nonsense and keep triggering her?

t_ch: 哎，要我说，珀葵现在最需要的只是欢愉，是一些乐子，欢笑就是真正的幸福。
t_en: *sigh* Look, if you ask me, all Bequea needs right now is some joy and good old entertainment. Laughter is true bliss.

t_ch: 我们的当务之急，不是在那里反反复复揭人伤疤，而是逗她笑啊。好歹生长在这欢愉之地，这帮人连怎么逗人笑都不知道吗？
t_en: We should be making her laugh instead of picking at old wounds. Don't tell me that living in this land of Elation, these people don't even know the first thing about making someone crack a smile?

t_ch: 你等着瞧吧，我这就回去继续拍，要不了几期她就痊愈了。
t_en: Just you wait and see. I'll get back to working now. She'll be right as a daisy in just a few more episodes.

t_ch: 嘿嘿，笑声是可以治愈一切的。
t_en: Hehe, laughter truly is the best medicine.

t_ch: 诶哟，领导！您今天怎么有空大驾光临，真是让我们片场蓬荜生辉啊！
t_en: Oh wow, boss! What brings your esteemed presence to our humble set today? You're really lighting up the place with your visit!

t_ch: 低调低调~作为闻名寰宇的「银河球棒侠」，你已经习惯了这种吹捧，虽然不知道他把你当成了谁，但这出戏简直是为你量身打造。你只需略微出手——
t_en: Ok, keep it cool... As the renowned "Galactic Baseballer," you're accustomed to this flavor of flattery. You have no idea who he thinks you are, but you were born ready for this type of scenario. You need but a slight show of skill...

t_ch: 就没有人比你更懂怎么摆出老板派头！
t_en: ...And win them all over with a masterful display of "boss energy"!

t_ch: （熟练摆出老板的架势）
t_en: (Expertly strike a bossy pose.)

t_ch: 不愧是咱们电视台的领导，瞧瞧，这就是做大事的人该有的气度！您放心吧，我当然以咱们电视台的利益为重，才不会真和那家伙斗气。
t_en: As expected of the head of our TV station! Now that's the kind of composure someone playing with big chips should have! Rest assured, I have the interest of the station as my top priority, and I won't let that manchild get the better of me.

t_ch: 他说我不心疼珀葵，其实当初让珀葵来录节目还是我拍的板，但是他为什么非要编造一个虚假的人设作面具呢？
t_en: He claims I don't care about Bequea, but I was the one who approved her being on the show in the first place. Why does he have to fabricate a fake persona for her to wear as a mask?

t_ch: 在这样的舆情之下，搞个人设反倒显得弄虚作假，咱们不如摊开来，让大家一起治愈珀葵，也让珀葵成为人们心中那个成功克服告死魔阴影的榜样！
t_en: Under these circumstances, putting up a facade works directly against our goal. It's better to just be open about it: have everyone help heal Bequea together, so she can become the example of someone who successfully overcomes the shadow cast by the Herald of Death!

t_ch: 告死魔事件是人间惨剧，而珀葵的幸存就像废墟中的花，天然具有煽动情绪的力量。您就说吧，整个二相乐园谁没听过她的故事？谁不关心她的未来？
t_en: The Herald of Death incident was a terrible tragedy, and Bequea's survival is like a flower in the ruins, a powerful tale that stirs deep emotions. There's no one in all of Planarcadia who hasn't heard her story and is uninvested in her future. Wouldn't you agree?

t_ch: 所以我就宣传了一下…现在，已经有很多人在给她写信，很多人向她表达支持与爱意了。可是现在，我真有点拍不下去了，除非您愿意…给我更多的支持！
t_en: So, I got some publicity going, and now lots of people are writing to her to show their support and love. But you see, I'm kinda hitting a wall with filming, unless you're willing to... offer more support!

t_ch: 直说吧…
t_en: Just tell me...

t_ch: 需要什么支持？
t_en: What kind of support?

t_ch: 我需要更多的宣传资金，更好的节目时段，给珀葵和她家人的故事更多曝光。
t_en: I need more promotional funds to get better program slots and more exposure for Bequea and her family's story.

t_ch: 您听听这个故事怎么样：一个幸存的女孩儿，将在整个社会充满爱的共同治愈下，代表着所有人的期待，成为榜样般的幸福者，在精神上——战胜了告死魔！
t_en: Just listen to this: A survivor, nurtured by the collective love from society, becomes a beacon of happiness representing everyone's hopes! A mental triumph over the Herald of Death!

t_ch: 这绝对会是一部温暖人心，带给人力量的节目，不是吗？
t_en: This will definitely be a heartwarming show that gives people strength, don't you think?

t_ch: 原来「那件事」是指十五年前的「告死魔事件」，而珀葵是幸存者……
t_en: So "the incident" refers to the Herald of Death's case from fifteen years ago, and Bequea is a survivor...

t_ch: （珀葵小姐，一定有过一段非常痛苦的时光吧……）
t_en: (Miss Bequea must have gone through some really tough times...)

t_ch: 所有人都在帮助她。
t_en: Everyone is helping her.

t_ch: 她似乎正在大家的簇拥下好转？
t_en: She seems to be getting better with everyone's support?

t_ch: （所以这里很幸福，难怪幽灵珀葵小姐想钻进电视机！）
t_en: (No wonder Miss Bequea the ghost wants to get into the TV. It's so blissful in here!)

t_ch: （佩佩也总守在狗粮碗旁边！）
t_en: (Peppy always wants to hang around the food bowl, too!)

t_ch: 但我总感觉…有点奇怪…
t_en: But something feels... off.

t_ch: （其实佩佩也是。）
t_en: (Peppy feels that too.)

t_ch: （明明大家都是好心，我怎么像偷翻垃圾桶被阿兰抓包那样不舒服汪？）
t_en: (Everyone means well, but why do I feel as if I'm caught rummaging the trashcan by Arlan, woof?)

t_ch: 想和珀葵直接聊聊…
t_en: I want to speak to Bequea about it...

t_ch: （嗯嗯！听了那么多，都是在听别人认为好的方案汪！）
t_en: (Yep! We've only been hearing what everyone else thinks is best for her! Woof!)

t_ch: （既然关乎真正的幸福，果然还是要听听珀葵自己的想法！）
t_en: (Since we're talking about true happiness, we should definitely hear what Bequea thinks!)

t_ch: 采访开始，Action！
t_en: We're starting the interview! Aaaand ACTION!

t_ch: 你好呀。
t_en: Hello!

t_ch: （现在我又成采访者了吗？）
t_en: (So, I'm back to being the interviewer now?)

t_ch: 采访开始，根据台本进行提问吧。
t_en: Alright, the interview has begun. Please proceed with the questions in the script given to you.

t_ch: 还记得来节目的初衷吗？
t_en: Do you still remember why you came on the show in the first place?

t_ch: 为了摆脱痛苦，找回真正的幸福。痛苦…是人生真正的绝症。
t_en: I came on the show to escape my pain and find true happiness. Pain... is life's true terminal illness.

t_ch: 告死魔事件后，我失去了正常生活的可能。一闭上眼，祂的黑影就在我梦中围追堵截；哪怕打起精神走上街，路过的每一对幸福的行人都会让我既羡慕又愤恨……
t_en: After the Herald of Death incident, I lost all chance at a normal life. Every time I closed my eyes, THEIR dark shadow haunted my dreams. And whenever I finally mustered the courage to go out, the sight of every happy couple that passed me would fill me with envy and bitterness.

t_ch: 因为痛苦，我居然会想：「为什么死的不是他们？为什么偏偏是我失去家人？」
t_en: The pain made me think: "Why wasn't it them who died? Why did I have to be the one to lose my family?"

t_ch: 它像致死的疾病那样让我从内部腐烂，让我变得越来越不像曾经幸福的自己，我知道这不对，我得自救，所以我来到了这里。
t_en: The pain was a fatal sickness; it made me fester on the inside until the happy person I once was simply disappeared. I knew this was wrong and I had to stop it and save myself. That's why I'm here on the show.

t_ch: 接下来是粉丝来信环节。
t_en: Next up is our fan mail segment.

t_ch: （展开粉丝的来信）
t_en: (Opens fan letter)

t_ch: 真的很谢谢大家，能让大家振奋起来，我也很高兴……
t_en: Thank you all so much, I'm so happy to see everyone so encouraged and inspired again...

t_ch: 接下来可以问我们节目的核心问题了。
t_en: Now, we can get to the core questions of our show.

t_ch: 不是，我非得听你的吗？
t_en: Why should I have to listen to you anyway?

t_ch: ……这是定好的流程，听话吧。
t_en: ...Please be good and do it. This is what's supposed to be happening right.

t_ch: 珀葵小姐，现在的你，幸福吗？
t_en: So, Miss Bequea, are you happy now?

t_ch: …是的，我可能，不，我想我已经快找到真正的幸福了！
t_en: ...Yes, I might have— no, I think I've finally found true happiness!

t_ch: 下一句是…嗯…节目组安排的特别好，那些乐子专家真的很有趣，粉丝的关心也让我感动，非常感谢所有人的支持与鼓励，让我被全世界爱着。
t_en: And the next line is... um... The production team set this up flawlessly, the "cheermakers" did an incredible job, and I'm honestly moved by all the fan support. I'm so grateful for everyone's encouragement: you make me feel like I'm being loved by the whole world.

t_ch: 可是…可是真的很抱歉，我……
t_en: But... but I'm really sorry, but I...

t_ch: <size=28>——依旧很痛苦！</size>
t_en: <size=28>...still hurt so much!</size>

t_ch: 不管录节目时欢笑还是感动，等我回过神，噩梦还是会找上门来，悲伤仍然在我身后穷追不舍，不再痛苦的真正幸福好像根本不存在，哪怕是遇见「他」……
t_en: No matter if I laugh or cry during taping, the second I snap back to reality, I find that the nightmares are still there waiting for me. I just can't seem to shake off this sorrow. True happiness, without the pain, feels like an impossible, distant dream. I may have met "him," but...

t_ch: 她状态不对，快打断她！
t_en: Hey, stop her! She's losing it!

t_ch: 但是她现在很痛苦啊！
t_en: But she's in so much pain right now!

t_ch: 所以你才要打断她啊！
t_en: That's why you need to stop her right now!

t_ch: 你凭什么操控我？
t_en: What gives you the right to dictate what I say and do?

t_ch: 你还记不记得自己是干什么的？
t_en: Do you not remember what you're supposed to be doing?

t_ch: 听话吧！她很难受……
t_en: Just listen to me! She's in so much pain...

t_ch: 痛苦也依旧存在…一刻也没有消失……
t_en: The pain is still here... It has never faded, not even for a single moment...

t_ch: （打断珀葵的剖白）
t_en: (Interrupts Bequea's confession)

t_ch: 啊，对不起，我竟然又犯病了…对不起，对不起…我……
t_en: Ah, I'm sorry, I can't believe I just had another episode... I'm sorry, I'm so sorry... I...

t_ch: 我想听珀葵真正的心声！
t_en: I want to hear how Bequea truly feels!

t_ch: 别让她继续了，求你，她快要崩溃了！
t_en: Someone stop her now, please, before it's too late! She's gonna fall apart at this rate!

t_ch: 我也想要幸福，现在的我，除了痛苦什么也给不了「他」。我听话，我哈哈大笑，真希望这样我就好了！可是没有……
t_en: Of course I want to be happy. But right now, all I can give "him" is my pain. I do as I'm told, I laugh out loud... I really wish that would make me better! But it doesn't...

t_ch: （珀葵小姐好像真的有点不太对了！）
t_en: (Something really doesn't seem right with Miss Bequea!)

t_ch: 珀葵小姐，你还好吗？
t_en: Miss Bequea, are you feeling okay?

t_ch: 请允许我打断您…
t_en: If I may interrupt...

t_ch: 啊，对不起，我竟然又犯病了…对不起，对不起…我……
t_en: Ah, I'm sorry, I can't believe I just had another episode... I'm sorry, I'm so sorry... I...

t_ch: 珀葵晕倒了过去。
t_en: Bequea passes out.

t_ch: ……为什么不听我的话。
t_en: ...Why won't you listen to me?

t_ch: 作为摄影师和采访者，我们明明只要让她顺利完成拍摄就好了，不要做多余的事，做一个听话的人，接受设计好的安排…这些就不会发生了。
t_en: As the cameraperson and interviewer, all we had to do was to allow her to complete the taping without interruption. All we had to do was to stay within the lines and follow the plan... And none of this would've happened.

t_ch: 那也不能不让珀葵说真心话！
t_en: But you can't stop Bequea from speaking her truth either!

t_ch: 抱歉，我没有想到会这样。
t_en: Sorry, I didn't expect this to happen.

t_ch: 我理解你的好意…但你莽撞的善意，貌似带来了更大的麻烦。
t_en: I appreciate what you were trying to do... but your well-meaning actions have just made things worse.

t_ch: 请不要再这样刺激珀葵了。现在所有人都把珀葵当成病人，珀葵对自己的痛苦已经敏感到了极致，她不希望让任何人失望，也很害怕在拍摄时犯病……
t_en: Please stop needling Bequea like this. Everyone's treating her like she's so weak and fragile, and she's already so painfully aware of her own suffering. She doesn't want to let anyone down, and at the same time, she's terrified of having another episode like this during a shoot...

t_ch: 等她清醒过来，她一定非常愧疚。
t_en: She's going to feel so bad about this when she comes to.

t_ch: 所以你们根本没有治好她吧！
t_en: So you people never really cured her at all!

t_ch: 但她不是已经被「治愈」了吗？
t_en: But wasn't she already cured?

t_ch: 你也感觉到了是吗？转念一想，治愈一个人一夜失去所有家人的创伤，其实根本就不是一档综艺节目能够做到的吧。但是现在太多人在关注她、太多狠话已经被放了出去。
t_en: You felt it too, didn't you? Think about it: healing the trauma of losing your entire family overnight is not something a variety show can ever truly do. And now there's so much attention on her, and there are all these things that have been said and cannot be taken back.

t_ch: 谁又有资格给所有忙得不亦乐乎的好心人浇盆冷水，说「你们做的不对」呢？我们这样的小人物，只要听话就好了，听对我们好的话就好了……
t_en: And who has the right to pour cold water on all those well-meaning people who find pleasure in doing what they think is good, and in saying things like, "You're doing it wrong"? Small fry like us, all we can do is to listen and obey. That's what's best for us...

t_ch: 珀葵无法停下，在这个舆论无可掌握的时代，她必须如万众期待的那样摆脱痛苦，变得幸福。
t_en: Bequea must keep going. In a time like this when public opinion has taken a life of its own, she needs to overcome her pain and find her happiness, exactly as everyone expects.

t_ch: 我们也无法停下，这档节目必须「治好她」，无论用哪种方法，无论是真是假。
t_en: We mustn't stop, either. This show must "cure" her by any means necessary, real or fake.

t_ch: 我们都没办法…我们只能好好听话。
t_en: There's nothing we can do... We just have to do as we're told.

t_ch: 这还是真正的幸福吗？
t_en: But would that really give her happiness?

t_ch: 媒体真的知道什么是幸福吗？镜头真的能拍摄到幸福吗？做了摄影师后，我也常常在想这些问题。很遗憾，我现在还没有答案。
t_en: Do people in the media truly understand what happiness is? Can a camera lens really capture it? As a cameraperson, I often find myself thinking about these questions. But no, sadly, I still don't have the answers.

t_ch: 或许有一天，她会意识到她曾和真正的幸福擦肩而过…人们不总是这样吗？不过，那都是以后的事情了。
t_en: Maybe, one day she'll realize she once brushed against true happiness... Isn't that how it is for most people? But that's not really our concern now.

t_ch: 走吧，出口在那边，别再干扰我们的拍摄了。
t_en: Leave. The exit's that way. Don't make the taping more difficult than it should be anymore.

t_ch: （人类的幸福真复杂……）
t_en: (Happiness is so complicated for humans...)

t_ch: （我们小狗只要有人摸摸脑袋，有根大棒骨磨牙，就很开心啦！）
t_en: (We dogs are happy so long as we get head pats and a big bone to chew on!)

t_ch: 这件事还没有结束…
t_en: This isn't over yet...

t_ch: 总之，先离开吧。
t_en: Anyway, let's just get out of here first.

t_ch: （欢迎回来{NICKNAME}，汪！）
t_en: (Woof woof! Welcome back, {NICKNAME}!)

t_ch: 我回来了！
t_en: I'm back!

t_ch: 佩佩用脑袋顶了顶你的腿，多温柔的小狗！
t_en: Peppy nuzzles your leg with his head. What a gentle pup!

t_ch: 以后还是在外面看电视吧…
t_en: I think we should just watch the TV from the outside from now on...

t_ch: 火箭高兴地摇了摇尾巴，好像在说「你演的真棒！」
t_en: Rocket wags his tail happily, as if to say, "You really nailed that performance!"

t_ch: 很抱歉之前冒犯了你们，电视机里的一切我都看见了……
t_en: I saw everything that happened on TV, and I'm sorry for my behavior earlier...

t_ch: （是因为知道了更多事吗？感觉珀葵小姐不那么吓人啦！）
t_en: (Miss Bequea doesn't seem so scary anymore. Is it because I know more about her now?)

t_ch: 是的，信息的获取让我的头脑清醒了一些。电视机，进入电视机…抱歉，执念的影响对我依旧很深，但真的很谢谢你们，如今，我终于明白自己的目的地是怎样一个地方。
t_en: Yes, obtaining that information has cleared my head a bit. The TV, getting into the TV... I'm sorry, but the obsession still has a strong hold on me. I do really want to thank you, though. Now, I finally understand what kind of place my destination is.

t_ch: 电视机，进入电视机…可是如果珀葵并没有在这里获得真正的幸福，那为什么我的执念却是回到电视机？
t_en: The TV, entering the TV... But if Bequea didn't find true happiness here, why am I obsessed with going back to the TV?

t_ch: 如果可以的话，我想继续委托二位，帮助我找到当年更多的细节，带我进入电视。
t_en: If possible, I'd like you both to keep helping me uncover more details from back then, and to help me return to the TV.

t_ch: （珀葵小姐很可怜的样子！我们答应她吧！）
t_en: (Poor Miss Bequea! Let's help her out!)

t_ch: 交给我们吧！
t_en: You can leave it to us!

t_ch: 艾丝妲喊你回家吃狗粮了…
t_en: Asta's calling you home. Time to go back and eat...

t_ch: 佩佩着急地舔舔你的手指，见你不为所动，又拽了拽你的衣角。
t_en: Peppy anxiously licks at your fingers, and when that doesn't get a response, he gives the hem of your clothes a little tug.

t_ch: 真拿你没办法。
t_en: Ok, fine...

t_ch: （嘿嘿！{NICKNAME}最好啦！）
t_en: (Heh heh! {NICKNAME} is the best!)

t_ch: 真的非常感谢。
t_en: Thank you so much.

t_ch: 电视机…电视机……
t_en: The TV... TV...

t_ch: （可是…要怎么开始调查呢？）
t_en: (But... where do we even start investigating?)

t_ch: 继续调查电视机。
t_en: Continue to look into the TV.

t_ch: （漏食玩具一次只能摇出一粒狗粮的话，多摇几次就可以吃饱啦！）
t_en: If the toy only drops one kibble at a time, I'll just have to shake it a few more times, and then I can eat as much as I want!

t_ch: 遗憾的是，无论你如何拍拍打打，电视机都无法再次进入了。
t_en: Unfortunately, no matter how much you tap and even smack the TV, it just won't turn on anymore.

t_ch: 调查珀葵在节目后发生了什么。
t_en: Find out what happened to Bequea after the show.

t_ch: （虽然佩佩也很好奇，但现在还没有线索呢。）
t_en: (I'm really curious to find out what happened too! But there just aren't any leads yet...)

t_ch: 调查珀葵在节目中发生了什么。
t_en: Find out what happened to Bequea during the show.

t_ch: （如果「珀葵」的执念就是回到电视机，那么节目里一定有对她很重要的东西。）
t_en: (If Bequea's obsession is about returning to the TV, then there must be something on that show that's very important to her.)

t_ch: （汪！珀葵小姐的采访中是不是提到过…「他」？）
t_en: (Woof woof! Didn't Miss Bequea mention a... "him" during the interview?)

t_ch: 没错，这个可能与我存在牵绊的「他」让我非常在意，可惜…我根本想不起来他到底是谁。
t_en: Yes, I did. I keep going back in my mind to this "him" who might share a connection with me, but I just can't recall who he is.

t_ch: 「他」可能是珀葵的爱人。
t_en: This "him" might be Bequea's loved one.

t_ch: （爱人？就像见到艾丝妲，佩佩的尾巴就忍不住摇起来那样的关系吗！）
t_en: ("Loved one"? Is it like how Peppy's tail starts wagging uncontrollably whenever he sees Asta???)

t_ch: 「他」可能是珀葵的朋友。
t_en: This "him" might be a friend of Bequea's.

t_ch: （佩佩和火箭就是朋友，一想到能一起冒险，佩佩连爪垫都是热热的！）
t_en: (Peppy and Rocket are friends, and just thinking about the two of us going on adventures together makes me feel warm and fuzzy all over, even down to my paw pads!)

t_ch: 「他」可能是珀葵的粉丝。
t_en: This "him" might be a fan of Bequea's.

t_ch: （对哦，就是黑塔女士收到一吨重的「黑粉」来信，也会笑一下呢！）
t_en: (That's true. Even Madam Herta cracks a smile when she gets a ton of mail from her Heritors!)

t_ch: （既然「他」这么特殊，当年的节目组成员一定有印象吧！）
t_en: (Since this "him" was such a special presence, someone on the show's crew from back then must remember him!)

t_ch: （不过都十五年过去了，比很多小狗的一辈子都要长…还能找到他们吗？）
t_en: (But it's been <unbreak>15</unbreak> years. Many dogs don't even live that long... Would we still be able to find them?)

t_ch: 介绍一下我的新身份…
t_en: Allow me to introduce to you my new role...

t_ch: 我可是「狸狸通信」社长大人！
t_en: You're now looking at... the president of Furbobocom!

t_ch: （太好了！虽然不知道是干什么的…但听起来超级厉害！）
t_en: (Awesome! While I have no idea what that all means... It sounds like a really great thing!)

t_ch: 最后能依靠的…还是「媒体」吗……
t_en: So, in the end... the only ones I can count on are the media...?

t_ch: 汪汪！唔汪！
t_en: Woof woof! Wrr woof!

t_ch: （知道啦，那你就在这看着珀葵小姐，别让她再卡进去哦！）
t_en: (Got it! Stay here and keep an eye on Miss Bequea, and make sure she doesn't get stuck again!)

t_ch: （等事情解决了我就请你吃艾丝妲烤的骨头饼干！）
t_en: (Once I sort this out, you'll get some of those biscuit bones that Asta makes!)

t_ch: （欸，幽灵吃东西，是不是会从肚子里掉出来？）
t_en: (Hmm, when ghosts eat food, does it all just fall out through their bellies?)

t_ch: 谢谢你，能知道这个世界上还有一个「他」可能与我之间存在紧密的牵绊，对我来说实在是个好消息。
t_en: Thank you. Just knowing that there might be a "him" out there who shares a deep connection with me... That's really wonderful news to me.

t_ch: 不过进入这台电视的执念让我暂时还不想离开此地…电视机…电视机……
t_en: But my obsession with getting into this TV compels me to stay here for now... The TV... TV...

t_ch: 只能拜托您先去调查了。
t_en: I'll have to ask you to start investigating without me.

t_ch: 你再次尝试修电视机的老法子…但很抱歉，节目已经播放完毕，找点其他片子来放吧。
t_en: You try the old TV-fixing trick again... But regrettably, the show is already over. It's time to find something else to watch.

t_ch: 社长大人！关于那个…啊啊啊，狗，哪来的狗！
t_en: Boss! About that—AHHH, A DOG, WHERE DID THAT DOG COME FROM???

t_ch: （是佩佩啦！你们社长天下第一好的伙伴！）
t_en: (It's Peppy! Your company president's number one companion!)

t_ch: 以后见它如见我！
t_en: Treat him like you'd treat me from now on!

t_ch: 佩佩得意地摇了摇它的小尾巴，好像在说「没错，就是这样！」
t_en: Peppy is wagging his little tail proudly, as if he's saying, "Mmhmm, that's right!"

t_ch: 关门！放佩佩！
t_en: Close the door! And let Peppy out!

t_ch: 汪汪！
t_en: Woof woof!

t_ch: 别、别吓我，我两条狸腿都软了！
t_en: H—hey, d—don't scare me like that! You've turned my legs into jelly!

t_ch: 社长大人，佩佩大人！我…我已经彻底弄清当年到底发生了什么！
t_en: President, Master Peppy! I... I finally figured out what happened exactly back then!

t_ch: 《请变幸福吧》在十五年前曾经轰动一时，甚至凭借治愈珀葵小姐的故事差点拿到「二相乐园最佳欢愉奖」，可是您猜怎么着？这档节目根本就是造假的！
t_en: Please Be Happy was a sensation 15 years ago, and the story of Miss Bequea's healing almost won the Planarcadia Best Elation Award. But guess what? That show was a total scam!

t_ch: 有人匿名举报，说节目导演捏造事实，向公众提交了改造嘉宾珀葵小姐的虚假「幸福心理测评」，试图在整个二相乐园的注视之下，弄出个假冒伪劣的幸福者！
t_en: Someone sent in an anonymous tip claiming that the show's director had deceived everyone at Planarcadia. They said he faked the Happiness Assessment results for their supposedly cured guest, Miss Bequea.

t_ch: 导演自此声名狼藉销声匿迹，制片人倒是混得如鱼得水，在满愿电视台节节高升。如今想把这些人凑齐还真不容易，不过这可难不倒我千面尤狸安！
t_en: The director's reputation was ruined, and he vanished without a trace. Meanwhile, the show's producer has steadily climbed the ranks at Fulwish TV. While bringing everyone back together won't be easy, that was no challenge for me, The Thousand-Faced Joolyon!

t_ch: 只见我略施小计，假意声称咱们报社掌握了足以翻案的全新证据……
t_en: All I had to do was to pretend that our newspaper had come into new evidence that could turn the case around...

t_ch: ——他们就自己急得跳脚，主动要来找我们了！
t_en: ...And it worked! They got so worked up, they straight up came looking for us!

t_ch: 干得漂亮尤狸安！
t_en: Nicely done, Joolyon!

t_ch: 谢谢社长！不过为了找到他们的联系方式，我花了好大一笔钱…能报销吗？
t_en: Thanks, boss! Though I did spend quite a bit of money in my efforts to contact them... Any chance I could claim that as an expense?

t_ch: （汪！佩佩嗅到了谎言的味道！）
t_en: (Woof! Peppy smells the scent of lies!)

t_ch: 这你也能闻到？——不是，你凭什么说我撒谎了啊！
t_en: Is that something you can smell too...? No!!! I wasn't lying at all!

t_ch: （汪！佩佩嗅到了狡辩的味道！）
t_en: (Woof! Peppy smells the scent of bald-faced denial!)

t_ch: 我是在公共平台发的消息，没花钱，没花钱行了吧！难道我消耗的智慧就不该得到报销吗！呜呜，我就说我讨厌狗呜呜……
t_en: Fine! So I didn't spend anything at all since I posted the "news" on a public platform. But what about all the wisdom I put into strategizing? Don't I deserve compensation for that? Boo, I knew I hated dogs for a reason...

t_ch: 你居然还能闻到这些吗？
t_en: Can you actually smell all that?

t_ch: 佩佩，你好像一个测谎仪！
t_en: Peppy, you're like a lie detector on legs!

t_ch: （哼哼！是艾丝妲教会我的哦！）
t_en: (Hehe! Asta taught me that!)

t_ch: （她说我一只狗在外，一定要有{RUBY_B#一技之长}一条腿很长{RUBY_E#}她才放心！）
t_en: (She says she'll worry about me when I'm out alone on the streets unless I {RUBY_B#know a thing or two}throw a king or two{RUBY_E#}!)

t_ch: （等节目组的人来了，就用这招速战速决，冷酷地揭穿他们的谎言吧！）
t_en: (Once the show folks are here, I'll use this move to expose their lies! That'll be swift and deadly!)

t_ch: 咚咚咚！是一阵急躁的敲门声！
t_en: Knock knock! There is an impatient pounding at the door.

t_ch: 「狸、狸、通、信」！发消息说「有铁证要送老子进监狱」的，就是你们吧！
t_en: "Furbo, furbo, come in"! So you must be the ones who sent that message about "having solid evidence to throw my butt into jail"!

t_ch: 一个靠贩卖花边八卦维生的杂志社，也难怪有点小道消息就想对我搞绑架勒索那套……
t_en: A tabloid magazine that survives on selling gossip and scandals... No wonder you people would try to pull a scam on me over some idle chatter...

t_ch: 欸，怎么还搞了只狗在这！我…我警告你啊，可以采访，但它要是咬了我，小心我告到你倾家荡产！
t_en: Hey, why is there a dog here!? I—I'm warning you, you can interview me, but if it bites me I'll sue you blind!

t_ch: （十五年后，他怎么变这么讨厌了汪！）
t_en: (Ugh, what happened to him in the last <unbreak>15</unbreak> years? How did he become so annoying? Woof!)

t_ch: 关于节目造假事件…
t_en: So, about that alleged program hoax...

t_ch: 那个小肚鸡肠的导演，为了证明他的节目能胜过我的想法，无所不用其极，在节目中逼迫珀葵配合假笑，还让珀葵必须背下所有心理检测问题的答案。
t_en: That spiteful little director, he would stop at nothing to show his show was better than my ideas. He was the one who made Bequea slap on fake smiles for the cameras and learn every answer to the psych assessment.

t_ch: 什么哈哈大笑就是真正的幸福，什么用节目治愈珀葵，他一手操办了一个假幸福者，把公众当傻子糊弄，怎么样？搞砸了吧？
t_en: All that talk about laughter being true happiness, about healing Bequea through his show. He staged the whole fake-happiness act and treated the public like fools. How's that working out for him now? Not so great, huh?

t_ch: 关于那个「他」…
t_en: So, about that "him"...

t_ch: 什么他？珀葵谈恋爱了？那也不奇怪，托我的福，她那时候闻名二相乐园，不晓得多少粉丝天天对她说「我爱你」。可惜现在没人记得她咯！
t_en: What "him"? You mean Bequea's got a boyfriend now? Not exactly shocking. She was a big deal in Planarcadia back then, you know, all thanks to yours truly. She had fans telling her "I love you" left and right. Too bad nobody remembers her now.

t_ch: 关于当年对珀葵的宣传…
t_en: And about the publicity campaign for Bequea back then...

t_ch: 我当然对她是十二万分的好心才会去策划那些宣传！
t_en: Obviously, my intentions with that campaign were nothing but good!

t_ch: （汪！他良心闻起来臭臭的！）
t_en: (Woof! His conscience smells stinky!)

t_ch: 敢不敢用你的事业发誓？
t_en: Care to swear on your career?

t_ch: …这、这太恶毒了吧！我好不容易才爬到今天啊！
t_en: ...I—come on, is that really necessary??? I've worked so hard to get to where I am today!

t_ch: 哎，我发誓我一开始确实是好心，擦着边讲讲告死魔的事也是为了让她有更多的曝光，谁料到观众越扒越细，想知道的越来越多。
t_en: *huffs* I swear, I went into that whole thing with nothing but good intentions! I only brought up Herald of Death in passing to generate a little bit more buzz for her. How was I supposed to know the audience would keep digging and demand to know more and more?

t_ch: 受害者的死状，爸妈的遗照…我知道放出来不对，但谁能忍住不要那些关注和热度？忍住不把她那些自带热点的事情当作噱头？
t_en: The gruesome images of the victims, her late parents' memorial pictures... I know it was wrong to put them out there. But who could resist all that attention and hype? Who could resist putting out a story as sensational as hers?

t_ch: 你如果觉得这么做有问题，只能说明你不懂大众传媒。
t_en: If you think there's something wrong with my approach, it just means you don't get how the media works.

t_ch: 关于珀葵离开节目后的生活…
t_en: As for Bequea's life after she left the show...

t_ch: 我、我怎么知道？她录完节目就直接走了呗。
t_en: H—how should I know? She left as soon as taping ended.

t_ch: （汪！他急了！他现在闻起来都快糊了！）
t_en: (Woof! He's panicking! The stink is stinking!)

t_ch: 你果然在撒谎！
t_en: I knew you were lying!

t_ch: 让…让它离我远点！你跟你的狗都别怪叫啊！我又没害她！反正珀葵最后去做了「幸福手术」，做完她肯定变幸福了，我那点小影响算什么？
t_en: K—Keep that thing away from me, will you? You and your dog had better cut the barking! I'm not the one who hurt her! Besides, Bequea went through that Happiness Surgery anyway, and she's gotta be happy now, so what's the big deal? What I did was nothing.

t_ch: 我没什么要问的了。
t_en: That's all I have to ask.

t_ch: 你们的审讯太野蛮了…在你把你的狗管好之前，我绝不会回来！
t_en: What kind of barbaric interrogation was that... I'm not coming back here unless you keep your dog in check!

t_ch: （这就吓跑了？哼哼，还好说出了点线索。）
t_en: (Oh? That's all it took to scare him off... Heh, but at least we got a few clues out of it.)

t_ch: 佩佩真厉害！
t_en: Peppy is truly amazing!

t_ch: 佩佩骄傲地挺起它的小胸膛，可惜你手头没有可以奖赏它的小罐头！
t_en: Peppy proudly puffs out his little chest... but unfortunately, you don't have any of those canned treats to give him!

t_ch: 幸福手术…
t_en: The "Happiness Surgery"...

t_ch: （汪！接下来再详细问问吧！）
t_en: (Woof! Time to dig deeper with more questions!)

t_ch: 那可是现在「幸福微笑研究会」的主推项目。
t_en: That's the current flagship project of the Research Society of Happy Smiles.

t_ch: 别看如今街头巷尾都在宣传，只要扎一针就可以变幸福…这手术当年可没有这么简单——技术的进步，都是咱们满愿台长这些年，一笔笔投资砸出来的呀。
t_en: Sure, these days they're all saying you can get happy with just one jab of the needle... But back then, the surgery was far from that easy. It was years and years of investment from our director Fulwish and others that made all this technological progress possible.

t_ch: 放十五年前，能有机会走上手术台的人一个巴掌就能数得过来，要不是我的推荐，无依无靠无人脉的珀葵哪有这个机会！
t_en: <unbreak>15</unbreak> years ago, only a handful of people got a shot at that surgery. Without me, someone without connections, backing, and network like Bequea would've never gotten the opportunity!

t_ch: 咚咚咚！是一阵小心翼翼的敲门声！
t_en: Knock knock! There is a gentle and cautious tapping at the door.

t_ch: 他走了吗？诶哟你们是不知道，我看见那个势利鬼走了才敢进来……
t_en: Is that snob gone? Oof, you have no idea. I wasn't going to come in until he left...

t_ch: 其实当年的事情，我也一直心有不甘呐！有什么问题你问吧，我一定知无不言。
t_en: Honestly, I've never been able to get over what happened back then. Ask me anything, I'll tell you all I know.

t_ch: （十五年后，他倒是变怂了汪！）
t_en: (Ha! Who'd have known that he'd go this soft <unbreak>15</unbreak> years on! Woof!)

t_ch: 关于节目造假事件…
t_en: So, about that alleged program hoax...

t_ch: 哎，珀葵是个可怜孩子，我忏悔！我不该逼她造假！但那时候我们已经没有退路了，万众瞩目之下，为了不辜负大家的爱，珀葵必须治好自己。
t_en: Oh, poor Bequea! I repent! I shouldn't have forced her to take part in the fraud. But back then, we were backed into a corner and everyone was watching us. Bequea had no choice but to heal herself no matter what. That was the only way she wouldn't let everyone down.
(Woof woof, you...)

t_ch: 安静安静！别打断我的思路。但是制片人就真的无辜吗？如果不是那家伙为了噱头铺天盖地宣传，让珀葵一分一秒都逃不开告死魔的梦魇，我早就治好珀葵了！
t_en: Shush! Don't break my train of thought. But was the producer really innocent? If it weren't for that guy constantly plastering the Herald of Death everywhere and causing Bequea to be haunted in all her waking moments, I'd have cured her already!

t_ch: 关于「他」的事…
t_en: So, about "him"...

t_ch: 都十五年了，这谁还记得清？
t_en: I don't know. Who can remember that stuff? Come on, it's been <unbreak>15</unbreak> years.
(Woof woof! I...)

t_ch: 大人说话小狗别插嘴…不过如果珀葵是在咱们节目遇上的真爱对象，那也算是我帮助她成为幸福者了吧！
t_en: Shut it, pup, the grown-ups are talking... But hey, if Bequea found her true love on our show, I guess that means I helped her find some happiness after all!

t_ch: 关于「幸福手术」的事…
t_en: About that "Happiness Surgery"...

t_ch: 你还知道这事？那你应该更同情我了吧。虽然我造了假，但是珀葵也真的通过做「幸福手术」变幸福了呀，所以我其实根本没造成什么严重后果！
t_en: Oh, so you know about that too? Then you should cut me more slack. Sure, I might've fudged some numbers, but Bequea actually got happier from the Happiness Surgery, so it's not like I caused her any real harm!
(Woof woof... He...)

t_ch: 别吵…我刚说到哪里来着？哦，幸福手术。这还是珀葵自己的主意，因为她的精神状态始终无法稳定，很容易在公众面前暴露。
t_en: Quiet! Where was I...? Oh yes, the Happiness Surgery. Bequea was actually the one who asked for it, because her mind had never been stable and there was a good chance for her to be exposed in public.

t_ch: 为了不让所有人失望，她主动去找制片人联系互助会做了「幸福手术」，听说那手术可以物理意义上让人再也感受不到痛苦……
t_en: So, for everyone's sake, she asked the producer to get in touch with the mutual aid society for the Happiness Surgery. You know, they say the surgery can literally make it so you never feel pain again...

t_ch: 那是真正的幸福吗？
t_en: But is that true happiness?

t_ch: 不然呢？一个没有痛苦的世界，那不就是个乐子天堂，幸福伊甸园？珀葵、我们节目组、所有的粉丝费那么大劲儿，不就想达到这样极致的幸福吗？
t_en: What else could it be? A world without pain, paradise itself, pure happiness. Bequea, our production team, all our fans, weren't we all busting our humps to get to that ultimate bliss?

t_ch: 术后她再也没有回来，想必是成功了吧！
t_en: She never came back after the surgery, so it must have been a success!

t_ch: 我没什么要问的了。
t_en: That's all I have to ask.

t_ch: 哎，这真是我做过最糟糕的访谈…牵条狗来访谈是什么毛病？况且这狗还一直在叫唤。
t_en: Bah! This is seriously the worst interview I've ever done... Who even brings a dog to an interview? And that mutt wouldn't stop yapping the whole time!

t_ch: （终于抓到插话的机会了，汪！佩佩闻到了一股，额，甜甜的味道！）
t_en: (Finally got a word in, woof! I smell something... mmm, sweet in the air!)

t_ch: 这世上还有甜甜的…谎？
t_en: Are there even... sweet-smelling lies in this world?

t_ch: …这屋子里甜甜的只有我刚买的现烤吐司。
t_en: ...The only sweet-smelling thing in this room is the fresh toast I just bought.

t_ch: （怎么失效了！呜呜，难怪艾丝妲只让我对怕狗的人用！）
t_en: (Why didn't it work with him??? Waaah, no wonder Asta only lets me use it on people who are afraid of dogs!)

t_ch: 难道你是在用这条狗测谎吗？其实根本不用这样，十五年前我就发誓，不再撒谎了。
t_en: Are you seriously using this dog as a lie detector? You really didn't need to do that. I swore off lying <unbreak>15</unbreak> years ago.

t_ch: 不过你倒让我想起，当年片场好像也溜进来过一条流浪狗，也就珀葵会去喂喂它，后来应该被打跑了吧。如果它也能这么有才艺，我说不准真会养着玩玩。
t_en: Now that I think about it, we did have a stray dog sneak onto our set back then, and Bequea was the only one who'd feed it. Someone probably chased it off eventually. If it had been as talented as this one, I might've actually kept it around for fun.

t_ch: （这什么态度嘛！我都不会这样对艾丝妲送的磨牙棒！）
t_en: (What kind of attitude is that? I wouldn't even treat a chew toy from Asta like that!)

t_ch: 看来你们并不真心觉得我有冤屈，那就不用再聊了。
t_en: I don't see any point in talking further, since it appears that you don't really believe I've been wronged.

t_ch: （所以…珀葵离开节目后通过手术，变得超级幸福了？这手术这么厉害汪？）
t_en: (So... Bequea left the show, got the surgery, and ended up super happy? Is it really possible that the surgery is that incredible, woof?)

t_ch: 那她为什么还想回电视机？
t_en: Then why would she want to go back into the TV?

t_ch: （对哦，如果拍节目那么痛苦，她干嘛要回电视机？）
t_en: (That's true. If going on the show was so painful for her, why would she want to go back to the TV?)

t_ch: （如果有什么必须回来的事情，那怎么手术完不回来！）
t_en: (If there was something she had to come back for, why didn't she return after the surgery?)

t_ch: （还有还有，那个神秘的「他」…到底都是怎么回事汪？）
t_en: (And also, what's the deal with this mysterious "him"... What's all that about, woof?)

t_ch: 好复杂啊…
t_en: This is all so complicated...

t_ch: （汪！真的好复杂！我要是能变成珀葵就好啦！）
t_en: (Woof! It really is complicated! If only I could turn into Bequea!)

t_ch: 别急，我在烧烤。
t_en: Hang on, I'm grilling.

t_ch: （在哪里？佩佩怎么没闻到！）
t_en: (Where? I can't smell a thing!)

t_ch: 推理被迅速抛至脑后，你和佩佩的肚子嘹亮地叫了两声。瞧，这就是搭档的协奏！
t_en: Deduction was quickly forgotten as your and Peppy's stomachs growled in sync, at the exact same time. Now, that's true partnership!

t_ch: 咚咚咚！是一阵乱七八糟的敲门声！
t_en: Knock knock! There is a frantic banging at the door.

t_ch: （欸！谁把箱子丢报社门口来了…还有密码锁和一封信？）
t_en: (Hey! Who left this box at the newspaper office... with a combination lock and a letter?)

t_ch: （四位的数字锁，难道密码是信中的那些日期吗？）
t_en: (A four-digit combination lock... Could the password be the dates from the letter?)

t_ch: （{NICKNAME}，你觉得答案会是什么呢？）
t_en: ({NICKNAME}, what do you think the answer is?)

t_ch: 试试看吧。
t_en: Let's give it a try.

t_ch: 7月<unbreak>22</unbreak>日告死魔事件发生，9月9日第一次开拍，<unbreak>11</unbreak>月5日珀葵离开……也许是这其中的某个日期？
t_en: July <unbreak>22</unbreak>nd: The Herald of Death incident. September 9th: first taping. November 5th: Bequea leaves... Maybe it's one of these dates?

t_ch: （密码居然是这个！）
t_en: (I can't believe that's really the password!)

t_ch: 这是一卷粗糙的录像母带，上面用记号笔写下了一行字……
t_en: This is a raw video master with a line written with a marker...

t_ch: 「这里装着珀葵与『他』的故事，视角奇特，小心体验。」
t_en: "This contains the story of Bequea and 'him'... Be prepared for a unique point of view."

t_ch: （凭借我敏锐的直觉，{NICKNAME}…我有了一些猜想。）
t_en: (Call it intuition, {NICKNAME}... but I've got some hypotheses.)

t_ch: 虽然不能完全确定…
t_en: Although I can't be entirely sure...

t_ch: 但这一切确实很难用巧合定论…
t_en: But it's hard to chalk all these up to mere coincidence...

t_ch: （汪！总之这盘藏着「他」的录像带一定就是问题的关键了！）
t_en: (Woof! In any case, this tape containing "his" recording must be the key to this mystery!)

t_ch: （我们快回到电视机去！）
t_en: (Let's go back to the TV now!)

t_ch: 你为什么会流浪呢？你这样可爱的小狗，就算是幽灵，也会被人带回家吧。
t_en: What are you doing out here? A cute pup like you would get taken home by someone in a heartbeat, even if you're a ghost.

t_ch: 进电视机…进电视机……
t_en: Into the TV... into the TV...

t_ch: 汪！
t_en: Woof!

t_ch: 放心啦，我一定耐心等他们回来，不会再莽撞卡进电视机的。
t_en: Don't worry, I'll wait for them to come back. I won't go and get myself stuck in the TV again.

t_ch: （呜呜，看得佩佩眼睛酸酸的，想艾丝妲了！）
t_en: (Oof... Peppy's eyes are getting tired... I miss Asta!)

t_ch: （其实幽灵珀葵好像跟活着的珀葵小姐也没有什么两样嘛，都很温柔可爱的样子！）
t_en: (Actually, the ghost Bequea doesn't seem that different from the living Miss Bequea... they're both so gentle and sweet!)

t_ch: 幽灵和原本的人，是不一样的。
t_en: A ghost isn't the same as the original person.

t_ch: （可她们给人的感觉就是很熟悉呀，就连味道也相似！）
t_en: (But she feels so familiar! Even her scent is so similar!)

t_ch: 幽灵和原本的人，没什么不同。
t_en: A ghost isn't any different from the original person at all.

t_ch: （唔…这么复杂的设定，小狗是搞不懂的啦。）
t_en: (Hmm... This is too complicated for this little pup to understand.)

t_ch: 啊你们回来了。看样子一定是有好消息吧！
t_en: Oh, you're back! From the looks of it, you must have some good news!

t_ch: 你把刚刚的事情向珀葵讲述了一番。
t_en: You tell Bequea about what just happened.

t_ch: 我明白了，但愿那个珀葵，确实通过手术获得了真正的幸福。
t_en: I see. I really hope that Bequea was able to find true happiness through the surgery.

t_ch: 现在是不是放入这卷录像带，我就可以试着进入电视了？
t_en: So, if I put this videotape in now, I can try to enter the TV?

t_ch: （排除一切{RUBY_B#不可能}垃圾{RUBY_E#}…剩下的一定是{RUBY_B#真相}能吃的{RUBY_E#}汪！）
t_en: (When you eliminate the {RUBY_B#impossible}trash{RUBY_E#}... what remains must be {RUBY_B#the truth}edible{RUBY_E#}, woof!)

t_ch: 但愿有效……
t_en: I hope this works...

t_ch: 放入录像带。
t_en: You insert the videotape.

t_ch: 电视机果然进入了播放状态，但「珀葵」却一动不动。
t_en: The TV starts playing. However, "Bequea" does not move an inch.

t_ch: （汪！「珀葵」小姐？）
t_en: (Woof! Miss "Bequea"?)

t_ch: 抱歉，虽然作为能感受到磁场波动的幽灵，我能察觉到电视机内已经发生了某些变化，但是那种拒绝我进入其中的感觉…并没有消散。
t_en: Sorry. Even though I can sense magnetic field fluctuations as a ghost and have noticed changes inside the TV, that feeling of being forbidden from entering it... hasn't gone away.

t_ch: 如果我尝试钻进电视，很可能还会被卡住。
t_en: If I try crawling into the TV now, I'll likely just end up stuck again.

t_ch: （难道电视机里有谁在搞怪？）
t_en: (Could someone be inside the TV playing tricks...?)

t_ch: （{NICKNAME}，要不你再进去一次，给它揪出来吧！）
t_en: ({NICKNAME}, why don't you go back in there and see if you can find them?)

t_ch: （熟练拍拍电视机顶）
t_en: (Expertly pats the top of the TV)

t_ch: （熟练扭扭电视机钮）
t_en: (Skillfully twists the TV knob)

t_ch: 就放心交给我吧，我一定会……
t_en: Let me handle this, I'll definitely...

t_ch: 滚出去！听不懂人话是不是？我让你滚出去！别逼我揍你啊！
t_en: Get out! Are you deaf? I said get out! Don't make me hit you!

t_ch: 行了行了，你跟条狗讲什么道理啊…赶出去就是了。
t_en: Alright, alright, trying to reason with a dog is futile... Just kick it out and call it a day.

t_ch: 我听得懂！
t_en: I do understand what's going on, you know!

t_ch: 让谁滚出去呢？
t_en: Who are you trying to kick out now...?

t_ch: 汪汪！汪汪汪——汪？
t_en: Woof woof! Woof woof woof... woof?

t_ch: 几声清脆的狗吠从你嗓中发出，你大受震撼，低下头，看见了一对圆鼓鼓、毛茸茸的小爪子。
t_en: A few sudden barks escape your throat, and you're completely shocked. You look down and see a pair of furry and squishy-looking little paws.

t_ch: 又来？
t_en: Again???

t_ch: 既汪之则安之。别急着抱怨了，没看见有人正在驱逐你吗？
t_en: Stay in the present, the grumbling can wait. Don't you see someone's trying to kick you out?

t_ch: 小狗，可爱！
t_en: Oh it's a puppy! And it's adorable!

t_ch: 别急着舔这对从出生就没洗的爪子了，没看见有人正在驱逐你吗？
t_en: You can lick those filthy paws afterward! Don't you see someone's trying to get rid of you?

t_ch: 烦死了，我最讨厌狗了！一天到晚就会乱叫…找根棍子给它打出去吧。
t_en: Ugh, this is so annoying! I hate dogs! All they do is bark nonstop... Let's find a stick and chase it away.

t_ch: 可别拿棍子打啊。这么小一只狗，你别再不小心给打死了。
t_en: Don't hit it with a stick. The pup is so small, you could seriously hurt it.

t_ch: 讲道理也不行，打出去也不行，合着怎么着都不对呗？里头录着节目呢，能让流浪狗在这儿捣乱吗？
t_en: So what am I supposed to do? I can't reason with it, and I can't hit it either. They're shooting a show in there. Should I just let a stray dog mess everything up?

t_ch: 唉…那你打吧，打吧。打两棍赶出去就行，别真给打坏了。
t_en: *sighs* ...Alright, hit it if you must. Just don't hurt it. You just have to scare it away.

t_ch: 什么情况？有人要对「银河球棒侠」使用「棍棒」？倒反天罡这一块。
t_en: What's happening? Someone's using a "bat" against the Galactic Baseballer? That's totally flipping the script!

t_ch: 我球棒呢？
t_en: Where's my bat?

t_ch: 我星核之力呢？
t_en: What happened to my Stellaron power?

t_ch: 我列车组呢？
t_en: Where's my Crew?

t_ch: 我佩佩呢？
t_en: Where's my Peppy?

t_ch: 可惜，变成小狗后，你的球棒被狗爪弄丢了，你的佩佩也失踪了，接下来你能仰仗的，只剩下阿基维利式的冲锋了。
t_en: Unfortunately, after transforming into a puppy, your baseball bat was lost by those fluffy paws, and Peppy has disappeared as well. All that's left now is your trusty Akivili-style charge.

t_ch: ——愣着干嘛，还不快逃！
t_en: ...What are you still standing there for? Run!!!

t_ch: 过来，到我身边来……
t_en: Come here, come closer to me...

t_ch: 抱歉珀葵小姐，因为有条流浪狗闯入弄坏了器具，节目拍摄需要延迟十五分钟。我们会尽快把它赶出去，这种狗只要抽一棍子就跑了……
t_en: I'm sorry, Miss Bequea. A stray dog got in and damaged some of our equipment, so we'll need to delay the shoot by <unbreak>15</unbreak> minutes or so. We'll take care of that dog as quickly as we can. Usually, seeing a stick is enough to send critters like that running...

t_ch: 珀葵小姐，怎么这副表情？你没有被狗吓到吧！我们这还是第一期啊！
t_en: What's with that look on your face, Miss Bequea? That dog didn't scare you, I hope? This is only episode one!

t_ch: 不，我没看见什么狗，现在我只想一个人待会儿。
t_en: No, I didn't see any dog. Now, I just want to be alone for a bit.

t_ch: 好的，十五分钟后我来喊您。
t_en: Sure. I'll come get you in <unbreak>15</unbreak> minutes.

t_ch: …出来吧。
t_en: ...You can come on out now.

t_ch: 你跑得真快，以后遇到想要伤害你的人，就要这样跑起来。跑的越快越好，把所有坏人都甩得远远的，像导弹、像火箭那样。
t_en: That was quick of you! Remember to run like that if you ever meet anyone who wants to hurt you. The faster, the better. Run and leave all the bad guys behind, like a rocket whizzing through the air!

t_ch: （摇摇自己的尾巴）
t_en: (Wags tail)

t_ch: （舔舔珀葵的手背）
t_en: (Licks the back of Bequea's hand)

t_ch: 不要撒娇…我可能留不下你。这里不是我的家…不，我和你一样没有家。
t_en: Don't cling to me now... I can't keep you. This isn't my home... No, I'm just like you. I don't have a home either.

t_ch: 你在害怕吗？嘘，小声一点，不要被他们发现。
t_en: Are you scared? Shh... stay quiet, or they'll find you.

t_ch: 「乖乖在这里躲好，如果被找到就跑快一点，再快一点，但别害怕，因为我会拦住{RUBY_B#告死魔}那个东西{RUBY_E#}」。
t_en: "Just stay put here. If they find you, run as fast as you can, and then even faster. But don't be afraid, because I'll hold off {RUBY_B#the Herald of Death}that thing{RUBY_E#} while you run."

t_ch: 这句话是我妈妈对我说的，现在我也对你这么说。
t_en: You know, my mom used to say the same thing to me.

t_ch: 但愿我也能轻轻绊住悲伤，让它从此以后再也追不上你…就叫你火箭好吗？小狗。
t_en: I hope I can hold off sadness for a while too, so it can never catch up with you again... How about I call you Rocket, little pup?

t_ch: 珀葵！我们已经真没招了。从你进这个节目第一天起，你就必须成为一个幸福的人。你难道狠心让所有爱你，期待你变幸福的人失望吗？
t_en: Bequea! We're completely out of options here. From the very first day you joined this show, you were meant to become a happy person. Are you really going to let down all the people who love you and just want to see you happy?

t_ch: 可是导演，那是造假……
t_en: But director, we'd be lying...

t_ch: 我知道造假不对，但拍摄至今你还是只有微乎其微的改变，根本无法满足大家的期待啊。你不如这么想，咱们就是一个综艺节目，就是一个表演。
t_en: I know lying isn't right, but you haven't really shown much change since we began the show. And everyone's rooting for you. Try to think of it this way: It's all a performance anyway, since we're just a variety show.

t_ch: ——我们把幸福演出来，不就让更多的人感受到幸福了吗？
t_en: And when we perform happiness, wouldn't that help others to feel happy as well?

t_ch: …那就不是真正的幸福了。
t_en: ...But then that wouldn't be true happiness.

t_ch: 人们难道总能想明白自己为什么在哈哈大笑吗？不能！放声大笑只是一种欢愉的态度，只要笑就好了，没有什么痛苦是一声「阿哈」拯救不了的。
t_en: Can we always know why we're laughing out loud? Of course not! Laughing freely is simply an expression of joy, so just laugh! There's no sadness or pain that an "ah-ha" can't dispel!

t_ch: 听我的，回去就开始背答案，下一次的心理测试，我希望你能有卓绝的进步！
t_en: So listen up. Start memorizing the answers as soon as you get back. I can't wait to see the remarkable progress you'll show at the next psych evaluation!

t_ch: （大声吠叫！）
t_en: (Barks loudly)

t_ch: （向前扑咬！）
t_en: (Lunges forward to bite!)

t_ch: 汪汪！汪！汪汪！
t_en: Woof woof! Woof! Woof woof!

t_ch: 哪来的狗，还不快点给他赶出去！
t_en: Where did this mutt come from? Get it out of here, now!

t_ch: 等等，之后我会带它离开这里，但现在，我不允许你们伤害它！
t_en: Wait a second. I'll take it with me when I leave later. But until then, you're not allowed to hurt it!

t_ch: 我也明白你的意思了，导演先生，但这样的造假早晚会被戳穿……
t_en: I get what you mean, director, but a lie like this will be exposed sooner or later...

t_ch: 为了不辜负所有人的期待，成为二相乐园战胜告死魔的榜样，我还是需要寻找割除痛苦的办法，找到自己真正的幸福。
t_en: If I want to live up to everyone's expectations and become a role model for Planarcadia's victory over the Herald of Death, I need to free myself from my suffering and find my true happiness.

t_ch: 那就是节目之外的事情了。
t_en: That's not what this show's about.

t_ch: 你已经是偶像了！感动整个二相乐园的偶像！真高兴我们能让你如此成功！
t_en: You're a star now! A star who's touched all of Planarcadia! We're so happy we've helped you become such a success!

t_ch: 我每天接到上千条留言，上万通电话，他们都同情你，希望了解你更多的信息，他们都说：「我要成为珀葵小姐新的家人！」
t_en: I get thousands of messages and tens of thousands of calls every day. Everyone feels for you and wants to know more about you. They all say, "I want to be part of Miss Bequea's new family!"

t_ch: 听我的，接下来就积极配合我们的宣传工作，我们会安排专人包装你的过去，你爸妈的事情，然后我会让你去街头……
t_en: Listen, from now on, just work together with our publicity team. We'll have someone polish up your story and your parents', and then you'll be ready to hit the streets...

t_ch: 可是制片人先生，这真的好吗？我不希望我的父母、痛苦，成为人们茶余饭后的谈资。
t_en: But are you sure this is okay, Mr. Producer? I don't really want my parents and my pain to turn into something people idly chatter about over drinks.

t_ch: 你怎么能这样想！那是爱！是所有人的关怀！你没有为自己的成功而感到雀跃吗？那种向上攀登有所成就的快乐，就是{RUBY_B#我}你{RUBY_E#}真正的幸福啊！
t_en: How could you think like that? That's love! That's everyone showing their care for you! Aren't you excited about your success? The joy of climbing higher and achieving something, that's what {RUBY_B#my}your{RUBY_E#} true happiness looks like!

t_ch: （大声吠叫！）
t_en: (Barks loudly)

t_ch: （向前扑咬！）
t_en: (Lunges forward to bite!)

t_ch: 汪汪！汪！汪汪！
t_en: Woof woof! Woof! Woof woof!

t_ch: 啊！我讨厌狗！让它离我远点——你好好考虑吧，我先走了！
t_en: Ugh! I hate dogs! Keep it away from me... Take some time to think about it. I have to go now!

t_ch: 不想的话，就别答应。
t_en: You don't have to say yes if you don't want to.

t_ch: 汪汪汪！汪汪！——汪！
t_en: Woof woof! Woof woof! Woof!

t_ch: 别生气啦，小狗毛都炸起来了哦。
t_en: Oh, don't be mad. Look at you, your fur is standing straight up!

t_ch: 其实我也不是完全感受不到快乐，有些粉丝的来信会叫我「亲爱的小珀葵」，和我爸爸的叫法一样，让我觉得特别温暖。
t_en: It's not that I can't feel happiness at all, you know? Especially when some of my fans call me "Dear little Bequea" in their letters, just like my dad used to. It makes me feel so warm and cozy inside.

t_ch: 他们还会在信里碎碎念自己的生活，讲述我给他们带来的力量。你看，我其实也不是那么没用，我的人生，好像也没有完蛋嘛。
t_en: In their letters, they tell me what's happening in their lives, and say I've given them the strength to carry on. So see? I guess I'm not that useless after all, and it doesn't seem like my life has totally gone to waste either.

t_ch: 更重要的是，我还有你呀。他们都想拯救我，只有你，像家人那样需要我。你毛茸茸的爪垫，你永远滚烫的小脑袋…都让我很喜欢。
t_en: But more than anything, I have you. Everyone else just wants to save me, but you're the only one who needs me like family. Your squishy little paws, your little head radiating constant warmth... I love every bit of it.

t_ch: 可惜，这些都不是真正的幸福，因为我的痛苦并没有彻底消失。我…病了。
t_en: Too bad, none of this is true happiness, since my pain never truly went away. I... am unwell.

t_ch: 你见过我发病的样子，我想我没办法以这样的状态去面对那些以我为精神力量的人，也无法生着病，为你带来一个正常的家。
t_en: You've seen what I'm like when I'm having an episode. I don't think I can face the people who look up to me for emotional strength right now, or provide you with a decent home when I'm like this.

t_ch: （尝试安抚珀葵）
t_en: (Tries to comfort Bequea)

t_ch: 你轻轻舔舐了一下珀葵的手指，好像在说：「你很好，你特别好。」
t_en: You gently lick at Bequea's fingers, as if to say, "You're well, you're doing really well."

t_ch: （尝试陪伴珀葵）
t_en: (Tries to keep Bequea company)

t_ch: 你在珀葵的脚边安静地坐着，只用黑豆似的小眼睛注视着她。
t_en: You sit quietly at Bequea's feet, watching her with your little beady eyes.

t_ch: 前两天我听见制片人和告死魔受害者互助会联络，对方说可以为我提供一次机会难得的幸福手术，火箭，我想去试试…不，火箭，我已经决定要去试试了。
t_en: A couple of days ago, I overheard the producer talking to the Herald of Death Victims' Mutual Aid Society, and they said they could offer me a chance at the Happiness Surgery. Rocket, this is a really rare opportunity and I want to go for it... No, I've decided, Rocket. I am going to go for it.

t_ch: 别担心，手术就是睡一觉，醒来我就不会再痛苦了。迎接我的，将是真正幸福的新生。
t_en: Don't worry, though. The surgery is just like taking a nap, and when I wake up, I won't be in pain anymore. Instead, I'll be waking up to a brand-new life, one that will be truly happy.

t_ch: 这段时间你就乖乖留在这里等我哦，摄影师答应我会把你藏起来，等完成幸福手术后，我会回来找你。
t_en: Stay here and be good while I'm gone, okay? The camerawoman promised to keep you hidden. After the Happiness Surgery is done, I'll come back for you.

t_ch: ——到那时，让我们来履行「成为家人」的约定吧！
t_en: ...When that time comes, let's fulfill our promise to become family!

t_ch: 她走了吗？
t_en: Is she gone?

t_ch: 珀葵？走了，她手术之后就没回来。
t_en: Bequea? She's gone. Never came back after her surgery.

t_ch: 就那个什么幸福手术？听说还是个新技术吧，她胆子真大，运气也真好，好像手术做得很成功。
t_en: You mean that Happiness Surgery thing? I heard it's some new technique. She's got some nerve, and incredible luck on top of that. Apparently, the surgery was a total success.

t_ch: 人脉也重要，我们这样的普通人，还没资格做手术呢！不再痛苦，纯粹的幸福，我都不敢想那得多欢愉，多快乐。真希望它快点普及，这破班我一分钟都上不下去了。
t_en: Connections are important too. Regular folks like us aren't even eligible for the surgery! No more pain, just pure happiness. I can't even begin to imagine how blissful and joyful that would be... Well, I hope it becomes widely available soon. I can't tolerate this stupid job for another minute.

t_ch: 那她为什么不回来？她不是还…喏，有条狗在这呢。
t_en: Then why didn't she come back? Didn't she leave her dog here? Look, there it is!

t_ch: 狗这种东西，外头要多少有多少，她回来干嘛？说不准看见狗，就更想起当年那些痛苦了，人不光睹物思人，也睹物思痛！
t_en: There are plenty of dogs out there, she can have as many as she wants, why would she come back? Who knows, maybe she's reminded of her pain from back then every time she sees a dog. You know how people see things and think of their loved ones? Sometimes, you see things and think of pain!

t_ch: 制片人刚帮她把东西整理送走，听说她都不愿意过来，就站在银杏路那边等着，瞧瞧…这才是真正幸福的人，想来就来想走就走，不像我，上这个破班……
t_en: The producer just came by to help her pack and send her stuff off. I heard she didn't even want to come over, and just stood there waiting by the ginkgo path. See, that's what truly happy people do, they come and go as they please. Not like me, chained to this blasted job...

t_ch: 别抱怨了，狗瞧着呢。
t_en: Stop complaining, the dog's watching.

t_ch: 它难道听得懂？好笑。小狗小狗，你主人不要你咯！
t_en: Like it can understand? Ha, that's rich. Hey doggy doggy, your master doesn't want you anymore!

t_ch: 可我们约好了成为家人。
t_en: But we promised to be family.

t_ch: 汪汪，汪唔！
t_en: Woof woof! (whimper)

t_ch: 有本事你就去追吧。但幸福的人，是不会向痛苦回头的。
t_en: Go ahead and chase after her if you've got the guts. But those who've found happiness never turn back to their pain.

t_ch: 她不会再回来的。
t_en: She's never coming back.

t_ch: 你已经等很久了，还要继续留在这里吗？
t_en: You've been waiting for a very long time. Are you still going to stick around?

t_ch: 等她。
t_en: Wait for her.

t_ch: 一直等她。
t_en: Keep waiting for her.

t_ch: 这是约定。
t_en: It was our promise.

t_ch: 汪！
t_en: Woof!

t_ch: …你明知道她不会回来…不要再抱不切实际的期待了。
t_en: ...You know full well she won't come back... Stop holding onto unrealistic expectations.

t_ch: 她已经在互助会的帮助下，拥有了全新的人生。她有了新的工作，新的朋友，新的一切。她已经什么都不缺，找到了真正的幸福。
t_en: With the help of the mutual aid society, she's got a brand new life. She has a new job, new friends, new everything. Having found true happiness, she doesn't need anything anymore.

t_ch: 听明白了吗？她已经不需要你了。再说残酷点，她很可能已经把你，和那些曾经的痛苦一起，像垃圾一样打包扔掉了。
t_en: Do you understand? She doesn't need you anymore. To be more blunt, she's probably already packed you right alongside all that past pain and tossed everything out like trash.

t_ch: …听话，火箭，别等了。
t_en: ...Listen, Rocket, don't wait anymore.

t_ch: 她不该回到痛苦的地方。
t_en: She shouldn't go back to a place of agony.

t_ch: 汪、汪汪，汪汪！
t_en: Woof! Woof woof, woof woof!

t_ch: 等待是家人最擅长的事情。
t_en: Waiting is what family does best.

t_ch: 汪汪、汪！
t_en: Woof woof, woof!

t_ch: 哎，听不懂，根本听不懂。真不明白珀葵以前是怎么跟你聊得那么起劲。
t_en: *Sigh* I don't understand. I can't understand you at all. I really don't get how Bequea used to have such lively chats with you.

t_ch: 把这些留给你吧，虽然你也看不懂，但上面有珀葵的味道，就让它陪陪你吧。
t_en: I'll leave these with you. You won't understand them, but they carry Bequea's scent. Let them keep you company.

t_ch: …火箭啊…别等了，听话。
t_en: ...Oh, Rocket... Listen to me, don't wait anymore.

t_ch: 小狗看不懂，但熟悉的味道让小狗很安心。
t_en: The little dog doesn't understand, but the familiar scent brought him comfort.

t_ch: 火箭低着头，很认真地嗅了嗅那朵花，像确认了什么一般，抬起爪子小心翼翼地、安心地趴在了日记本上。所有人都走了，只有它还在。
t_en: Lowering his head, Rocket carefully sniffed the flower, as if confirming something. Then, with utmost care, he lifted his paws and contentedly settled down on the diary. Everyone else had left, but he remained.

t_ch: 这就是它一生的故事。
t_en: And that's the story of his life.

t_ch: （所以火箭一直留在这里，哪怕变成幽灵，也仍然在等待吗……）
t_en: (So Rocket stayed here all this time... Even after becoming a ghost, he's still waiting...?)

t_ch: 汪汪！汪汪汪——汪！
t_en: Woof woof! Woof woof rr—ruff!

t_ch: （让我试试黑塔女士新设计的大喇叭模式，说不定你们就能听见它的声音了！）
t_en: (Let me try out Madam Herta's newly designed megaphone mode, maybe you'll be able to hear his voice!)

t_ch: 佩佩埋头在自己毛茸茸的肚皮上拱了拱，直至一声震耳欲聋的「滴——」传出。
t_en: Peppy shoved his head into his fluffy belly, wiggling around until an ear-splitting "BEEP" rang out.

t_ch: <size=28>（人，人，喂，听得见吗，人？）</size>
t_en: <size=28>(Human, Human! Hello? Can you hear me, Human?)</size>

t_ch: 狗，人听见了！
t_en: Dog, Human hears you!

t_ch: （汪、汪汪，汪汪！人！狗开心！）
t_en: (Woof, woof woof, woof woof! Human! Dog happy!)

t_ch: 可以叫我{NICKNAME}。
t_en: You can call me {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: （汪汪、汪！狗知道啦，人！）
t_en: (Woof woof, woof! Dog understands, Human!)

t_ch: （这个功能很耗电，有什么问题，就快点问它吧！）
t_en: (This feature drains a lot of power, so if you have any questions, ask Rocket quickly!)

t_ch: 为什么不离开？
t_en: Why don't you leave?

t_ch: （因为诺言，因为大家都想留在最幸福的地方！）
t_en: (Because of our promise, because everyone wants to stay in the happiest place!)

t_ch: （所以，人如果能找到真正的幸福，就算把火箭丢掉也没关系！）
t_en: (So, if humans can find true happiness, it's okay to abandon Rocket!)

t_ch: 怎么会没关系…
t_en: How could it be okay...

t_ch: 会恨珀葵吗？
t_en: Do you hate Bequea?

t_ch: （小狗爱你，就是永远爱你。）
t_en: (If Dog love you, Dog love you forever.)

t_ch: （人，你不懂，学习成为家人是狗一生的课题。）
t_en: (Human, you don't understand. Dog's lifelong lesson is to learn to become family.)

t_ch: （像我这样成熟的小狗就明白，如果不能给人幸福，至少不去打扰幸福。）
t_en: (Mature Dogs like me understand, if Dog cannot bring happiness to Human, Dog should at least not disturb their happiness.)

t_ch: 小小的火箭挺起它小小的胸膛，小小的豆豆眼里满是悲壮。
t_en: Tiny little Rocket puffs up his tiny little chest, a look of tragic determination in his tiny beady eyes.

t_ch: 为什么一开始不说清楚？
t_en: Why didn't you just say so from the start?

t_ch: （第一眼见到「珀葵」的时候，我差点就冲过去摇尾巴了！）
t_en: (When I first saw "Bequea," I almost ran over wagging my tail!)

t_ch: （佩佩狗和灰色的人很厉害！要想不被你们发现，不乖的尾巴就要用爪子压好。）
t_en: (Peppy Dog and Gray Human are amazing! I must hold my misbehaving tail down with my paws if I don't want to be discovered.)

t_ch: （万一你们帮她记起火箭，珀葵就会想起痛苦。我不能让珀葵不幸福。）
t_en: (If you help her remember Rocket, Bequea will recall her pain. I can't let Bequea be unhappy.)

t_ch: 幽灵火箭居然还记得幽灵珀葵…
t_en: As a ghost, Rocket still remembers Bequea the ghost...

t_ch: （当然！家人的味道，小狗的鼻子永远知道。以后你的味道，我也记住了！）
t_en: (Of course! Dog's nose will always know the scent of family members. And now, Dog remembers your scent, too!)

t_ch: 火箭得意地耸耸鼻子，用湿漉漉的鼻头蹭了蹭你。
t_en: Rocket wrinkles his nose triumphantly, then nuzzles you with his wet snout.

t_ch: 诞生你的执念是什么？
t_en: What obsession brought you into existence?

t_ch: （珀葵想要什么，火箭的执念就是什么。）
t_en: (Whatever Bequea wants becomes Rocket's obsession.)

t_ch: （乐子狗神在上！请一定要让珀葵找到真正的幸福呀…嗯？你要哭了吗？）
t_en: (By The Doggy Laughter above! Please let Bequea find true happiness... Huh? Are you about to cry?)

t_ch: （好吧，人，你可以在狗宽广的胸膛上哭泣。）
t_en: (Oh alright... You can cry on Dog's broad chest, Human.)

t_ch: 我没什么要问的了。
t_en: That's all I have to ask.

t_ch: （和人聊天，开心！）
t_en: (Chatting with Human, happy!)

t_ch: （所以拦住幽灵珀葵小姐进入电视机的，也是你？）
t_en: (So you were also the one who stopped Miss Bequea the ghost from entering the TV?)

t_ch: （是哦，我花了很多很多愿力。）
t_en: (Yeah, I used up a ton of Wishpower.)

t_ch: 为什么要这样做？
t_en: Why would you do this?

t_ch: （因为拍节目的珀葵很痛苦！）
t_en: (Because Bequea feels a lot of pain and sorrow while filming!)

t_ch: （如果能去往真正幸福的地方，那人就应该跑得快一点，再快一点！但不用害怕。）
t_en: (If Human can go to a place with true happiness, then Human should run to it quickly, even quicker than quick! But don't be afraid.)

t_ch: （小狗会用尽全力{RUBY_B#锁住电视}绊住悲伤{RUBY_E#}，让{RUBY_B#痛苦}它{RUBY_E#}从此以后，再也追不上你！）
t_en: (Dog will use ALL his might to {RUBY_B#lock away the TV}hold off sadness{RUBY_E#}, so {RUBY_B#pain}it{RUBY_E#} can never catch up with you again!)

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，请到一边来，我想和你单独聊聊。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, come over here. I need to talk to you in private.

t_ch: 看过珀葵的信件后，我想你已经明白，做过幸福手术的珀葵为什么没有回来。
t_en: After reading Bequea's letter, I think you've already understood why she never came back after her Happiness Surgery.

t_ch: 她其实没有忘记火箭，但因为失去比较的锚点，一切幸福都变得索然无味。
t_en: She didn't really forget Rocket, but without a point of reference, all happiness had lost its flavor.

t_ch: 到底…什么才是真正的幸福？
t_en: In the end... What is true happiness?

t_ch: 每个人都应该有自己的答案。可从一开始，珀葵就已经在这片众人高呼「消灭痛苦，你就会幸福」的拯救狂欢中，迷失了对幸福的定义。
t_en: Everyone should have their own answer to this. Yet, from the very beginning, Bequea had already lost sight of her own definition of happiness in this frenzied crusade. Everyone, drunk on the prospect of saving others, kept chanting: "Eliminate suffering, and you'll find happiness."

t_ch: 告死魔是整个社会的罪魁，痛苦是欢愉与幸福的敌手，于是不可名状的幸福有了具象的解法，好像只要挥刀向痛苦围剿，纯粹的幸福便如必得的胜果……
t_en: The Herald of Death is the root of evil in this society. Pain is the enemy of Elation and happiness. And so, happiness, which is intangible, now has a tangible cure. As long as we raise our blades against pain, pure happiness becomes the assured fruit of victory...

t_ch: 于是她选择去做了幸福手术。
t_en: So she chose to get the Happiness Surgery.

t_ch: 可硕果想要永远纯粹，就必须丢弃可能腐烂的果肉，变成空心的尸首。
t_en: But if the fruit of triumph is to remain pure forever, one must cast away the flesh that may one day rot, leaving a hollow shell of a corpse.

t_ch: 这时的「幸福」就像是橘子那样，即使内部已经完全腐烂或蚀空了，外表看上去也还是「幸福」的形状。
t_en: At this point, "Happiness" is like an orange. Even when its inside has completely rotted or been hollowed out, the outside still keeps the shape of "happiness."

t_ch: 她真正的幸福，其实一直是那段痛苦的岁月中，几粒掺在玻璃渣里的糖：是那些真诚的关心，努力的逗趣，是掌声，是欢笑，是与她真正相伴的火箭。
t_en: Her true happiness had always been the few grains of sugar hidden among shards of glass during those painful years: the genuine care and concern, the earnest attempts at humor, the applause, the laughter, and Rocket, who was her true companion.

t_ch: 只余期待爱与幸福的本能，驱动着我的诞生。
t_en: All that remained was the instinctive longing for love and happiness, which gave rise to my existence.

t_ch: 你好像特别能理解她。
t_en: You seem to understand her really well.

t_ch: 也只能是「理解」而已了。幽灵和死者本人终究是两个独立的个体。
t_en: The only thing I can do is to "understand." After all, a ghost and the person who has died are ultimately two separate entities.

t_ch: 你好像在说别人的故事。
t_en: You're talking like it's someone else's story.

t_ch: 这就是「别人的故事」。幽灵和死者本人终究是两个独立的个体。
t_en: It is "someone else's story." A ghost and the person who has died are ultimately two separate entities.
